title: Maintenance Operations System
description: A workflow-oriented web application concept for maintenance reporting, review, and operational coordination.
summary: A fullstack operations system concept for maintenance teams that need clearer reports, status tracking, and review flows.

sections.heading: Overview
body: This project is based on common maintenance workflows: reports, status updates, review steps, documentation, and follow-up actions.
The concept focuses on turning scattered operational activity into a structured web application that teams can use repeatedly.

sections.heading: Challenge
body: Maintenance workflows can become difficult to follow when data, evidence, approvals, and communication are split across different tools.
The interface has to support detailed work without making daily users feel slowed down.

sections.heading: Approach
body: I structured the concept around clear statuses, report detail pages, dashboard summaries, and predictable action areas.
The fullstack direction includes practical data modeling, API routes, permissions, and frontend states that support real operational use.

sections.heading: Outcome
body: The outcome is a project direction that can support reporting, reviewing, and tracking maintenance work from one place.
It is a strong candidate for a real case study once final project details and screenshots are ready.
